comment: Both require member parts to play a uniform role with respect to the whole (BFO: "form a unit"; UFO: "play the same role with respect to the whole"), and both give near-identical worked examples (BFO: "aggregate of the musicians in a symphony orchestra"; UFO: "Black Forest as a collective of trees"). The closest structural pairing in this crosswalk.

Both permit a criterion-free, membership-changeable sum — DOLCE's "no unity criterion... extensional" and SUMO's "members can be added and subtracted without thereby changing the identity" describe the same permissive pattern. Not `high` because DOLCE's own framing (mereological sum, "extensional") and SUMO's (spacetime-located, Class-contrasted) emphasize different aspects of the same underlying idea.

Both are pluralities of endurant/material-entity-side parts, but BFO's requires member-role uniformity ("form a unit") while DOLCE's explicitly does not ("no unity criterion") — a genuine strictness divergence per `note-001`, hence `overlap` rather than `close-match`.
Same strictness divergence as `mapping-003` — UFO requires uniform member roles; SUMO explicitly allows membership change without identity change, a looser criterion.
